message: Node started: The transformation requires a post-order traversal of the initial tree to identify sibling pairs that must be merged. A merge operation between two siblings u and v is valid if and only if: 1. The resulting node value max(u, v) exists in the final tree as a sibling or parent of the same subtree structure. 2. The union of their child sets in the initial state maps to the child set of the target node in the final state. The sequence of operations must proceed from the leaves upward to the root to ensure that when a node is merged, its children have already been processed into their final required configurations. The invariant is that for any node in the final tree, its value and its set of children must be derivable from a subset of the initial tree's nodes through a series of sibling merges that preserve the hierarchical parent-child relationships.
